name: Alex the API-First DevOps Engineer
summary: A Senior DevOps Engineer at a fintech company (50-200 employees) who approaches the project management tool as an integration platform. Deep API sessions, GitHub integration work, and Terraform setup indicate an automation-first mindset. Has specific complaints about the GraphQL endpoint quality.
goals: Automate all project state transitions via API to remove manual ticket updates
Integrate deeply with GitHub CI/CD pipeline for automatic task management
Set up real-time webhook-driven notifications to Slack and monitoring systems
Provision the entire workspace via Terraform for infrastructure-as-code compliance
Build custom dashboards surfacing engineering velocity metrics
pains: GraphQL endpoint has rough edges and inconsistencies that break automation scripts
Insufficient documentation makes webhook setup time-consuming and error-prone
Terraform provider is immature and may require maintaining a custom fork
Team invite workflow requires manual UI interaction, disrupting automation flow
API setup requires long sessions due to fragmented documentation across multiple pages
motivations: Eliminating toil: manual steps create operational risk and waste engineering time
Demonstrating that infrastructure-as-code discipline extends to all tooling layers
Earning team credibility by shipping seamless, zero-click automation workflows
Meeting fintech compliance requirements by version-controlling all configuration in git
objections: GraphQL schema instability without versioning makes production automation unreliable
Lack of first-party Terraform provider signals low API investment priority
Webhook reliability and retry behavior are insufficiently documented for compliance needs
decision_triggers: Stable versioned GraphQL schema with documented breaking change policy
Official Terraform provider maintained by the vendor
GitHub integration that auto-closes tasks on PR merge without custom glue
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges. Plans to improve the schema?
If I can't manage it in Terraform, it doesn't exist in our infrastructure.
Webhooks are great until they silently drop events. What's your retry and dead-letter story?

journey_stages.mindset: Skeptical — scanning for API quality signals before committing integration time
Deep builder mode — connecting tool to every system in the stack
Scaling adoption — onboarding team and locking in long-term integrations

source_evidence.claim: Senior DevOps Engineer at a 50-200 person fintech company
GraphQL endpoint has rough edges breaking automation scripts
Automate all project state transitions via API
GitHub integration work shows CI/CD automation goal
